REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT 101 LOMBARD STREET CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, CITISCAPE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC DEFENDANTS 101 LOMBARD STREET CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION AND CITISCAPE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLCS MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: ANGELA POURTABID, CSR# 137114, 415.601.7054, APOURTABID@GMAIL.COM. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAIEND WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. (OPPOSITION 4:5-6). DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING AND ELDER ABUSE FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS GIVING RISE TO THESE CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON "SUBSEQUENT AND CONTINUING ACTS" (OPPOSITION 7:23) NOT ARISING OUT OF THE REFUSAL TO PAY ON INSURANCE CLAIM (ADDRESSED IN SMALL CLAIMS ACTION). LINCOLN PROPERTY CO., N.C., INC. V. TRAVELERS INDEMNITY CO. (2006) 137 CAL.APP.4TH 905. PLAINTIFF HAS TWENTY DAYS TO FILE ANY AMENDED PLEADINGS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Transferred to be heard in department 501 on the same date at 9:30 a.m. with the motion to strike.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Defendants 101 Lombard Street Condominium Association And Citiscape Property Management Group, Llcs Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff?S Complaint, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration Of Kara L Wild, Proposed Order
Off Calendar Oct-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
James Farinaro, 510.533.1200 of the Law Offices of James Farinaro appearing vai Zoom for Plaintiff Sharon Schwartz.
Jenny Chu, 510.841.7777 of Pacific Law Partners, LLP appearing via Zoom for Defendant State Farm.
Gagik Stepanyan, (626) 792-2400 of Hosp Gilbert & Bergsten appearing via Court call for Defendant Ideal Restoration, Inc.
Reporter: Angela Pourtabid, CSR# 137114, 415.601.7054, apourtabid@gmail.com.

Discussed. Mr. Farinaro ask to the Court clarification regarding the time to amend. The Court states that Plaintiff has ten days from notice of entry of order to amend.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT 101 LOMBARD STREET CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, CITISCAPE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC Defendants 101 Lombard Street Condominium Association And Citiscape Property Management Group, Llcs Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's complaint is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, supporting malice, oppression or fraud as to the moving defendant. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Angela Pourtabid, CSR# 137114, 415.601.7054, apourtabid@gmail.com. (D501)

Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
James Farinaro, 510.533.1200 of the Law Offices of James Farinaro appearing vai Zoom for Plaintiff Sharon Schwartz.
Jenny Chu, 510.841.7777 of Pacific Law Partners, LLP appearing via Zoom for Defendant State Farm.
Gagik Stepanyan, (626) 792-2400 of Hosp Gilbert & Bergsten appearing via Court call for Defendant Ideal Restoration, Inc.
Reporter: Angela Pourtabid, CSR# 137114, 415.601.7054, apourtabid@gmail.com.

Argued (See Reporter's Transcript for complete details). Plaintiff request twenty days to file any amended pleadings. The Defendants do not object.

The Court rules as follows: Demurrer To Complaint is SUSTAIEND without leave to amend as to the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. (Opposition 4:5-6). Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing and Elder Abuse for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to these causes of action based on "subsequent and continuing acts" (Opposition 7:23) not arising out of the refusal to pay on insurance claim (addressed in Small Claims action). Lincoln Property Co., N.C., Inc. v. Travelers Indemnity Co. (2006) 137 Cal.App.4th 905. Plaintiff has twenty days to file any amended pleadings. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
